scope: Theme Starter first-party theme source and repository tooling

measuredRules.evidence: The current Starter source has no empty blocks, so the recommended rule remains enabled while the exact evidence probe guards the measured zero.

The sole diagnostic is the conventional catch binding in getRuntimeRouteHref; keeping that compatibility catch unchanged avoids a release-bearing theme-source edit while exact identity prevents movement or growth.
The current Starter source has no useless assignments, so the recommended rule remains enabled while the exact evidence probe guards the measured zero.

noGrowth.policy: Root-level and scripts tooling keep every recommended rule enabled. Theme source excludes only the single measured no-unused-vars occurrence; its path, rule, message, full lexical owner, full-file AST path with array ordinals, and normalized diagnostic-context fingerprint must match exactly and may only shrink relative to the merge base. All other recommended diagnostics fail CI.

inlineConfiguration.policy: Source comments cannot disable or reconfigure ESLint rules. The executable proof loads the real project configuration and confirms a used no-undef directive neither suppresses nor downgrades the severity-2 diagnostic.

eslint.removalCondition: Remove the no-unused-vars exclusion after an intentional theme cleanup removes the exact catch-binding diagnostic without changing the Press theme contract.

diagnosticBaseline.identity: path, rule, messageId, message, full lexical owner, full-file AST path with array ordinals, normalized diagnostic-context fingerprint, and duplicate occurrence
diagnosticBaseline.bootstrap: The initial baseline must be a subset of diagnostics independently recomputed from the exact merge-base theme source; a same-count owner or context swap is forbidden.

noGrowth.policy: New candidate files must pass Prettier. A changed legacy file must be formatted and removed from the baseline; only exact-content renames may transfer baseline ownership.

htmlSinks.policy: Every known HTML write or parser sink is classified by path, full lexical owner, full-file AST path with array ordinals, kind, normalized semantic AST fingerprint, disposition, and rationale. Starter's setHtml wrapper is not a sanitizer: its implementation and all five current callers are classified separately by actual input provenance. The first policy must be a subset of sinks independently recomputed from exact merge-base source; approved keys may then only shrink and established wrapper names cannot be removed or renamed. Opaque computed assignments, loop targets, calls, tags and constructors; wrapper/native/document aliases; dynamic parser MIME values; default-parameter and destructured reflection aliases; bind/call/apply/Reflect.get helper forms; single and bulk descriptor lookup APIs; legacy __lookupSetter__; and opaque payloads or descriptors all fail closed.
